chunks.text: ORDER SHEET IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH BENCH AT SUKKUR C. P No. D – 304 of 2025 Date of hearing Order with signature of Judge Hearing of case (Priority) 1. For orders on office objection at flag ‘A’ 2. For hearing of CMA No.1402/2025 (S/A) 3. For hearing of main case 27.03.2025 Mr. Aijaz Ali Bhutto, Advocate along with Petitioners Mr. Saeed Ahmed Wassan, Assistant Advocate General Sindh Mr. Mansoor Ali Shaikh, Deputy PG for State >>>>>..<<<<< Inspector Niaz Ali Lashari, Investigating Officer Crime No.02 of 2025 of Police Station, Taggar, District Naushahro Feroze is present submits that after the investigation and recording statement of petitioner No.1 namely, Mst. Zoya, who has not supported the version of the complainant as set out in the FIR by complainant, namely Shoaib Ali / Respondent No.6., he is of the opinion that the case is liable to be disposed of as cancelled under ‘C’ class. He further submits that in the light of statement of petitioner No.1, he will file the final report before the concerned Court of law having jurisdiction for disposal of the case under ‘C’ class within 15 (fifteen) days. After such statement of the Investigating Officer, Counsel for the petitioners seeks disposal of this petition as the Investigating Officer is going to submit report by cancelling the FIR under ‘C’ class and further prays that directions may be issued to the official respondents for providing necessary protection to the petitioners. Since the Investigating Officer after completing the investigation is going to file report under ‘C’ class for cancellation of the FIR, therefore, he is directed to file such report before the concerned Magistrate within 07 days, as suggested by him.

After such statement of the Investigating Officer, Counsel for the petitioners seeks disposal of this petition as the Investigating Officer is going to submit report by cancelling the FIR under ‘C’ class and further prays that directions may be issued to the official respondents for providing necessary protection to the petitioners. Since the Investigating Officer after completing the investigation is going to file report under ‘C’ class for cancellation of the FIR, therefore, he is directed to file such report before the concerned Magistrate within 07 days, as suggested by him. In view of the above, the instant Constitutional Petition is disposed of. However, the police officials are directed to provide legal protection to the petitioners in accordance with the law. Judge Judge ARBROHI
